message:
feat(raptorq): add schema-aligned failure context to all builder tests

Every RaptorQ builder test (sender, receiver, roundtrip, security,
cancellation, oversized, empty, symbols) now emits structured failure
context with scenario_id, seed, parameter_set, and replay_ref fields
when assertions fail. This makes CI failures immediately actionable:
the failure message tells you exactly which replay catalog entry to
consult and which parameters to reproduce with.

A new `builder_failure_context()` helper centralizes the format string
construction so all builder tests follow the same schema.

Also fixes a Clippy lint in gf256.rs (`c as u64` → `u64::from(c)`)
to eliminate a truncation warning on the nibble table exhaustive check.

The D9 replay catalog gains 8 new entries covering the full builder
test surface (send-transmit, security-send, oversized-error,
cancelled-send, roundtrip, receiver-insufficient, send-empty,
send-symbols), each linked to their unit test and E2E conformance script.

The unit test matrix documentation is updated to reflect that builder-path
tests now have full schema-aligned context and the remaining `partial`
status is limited to non-builder families.

Changes:
- src/raptorq/tests.rs: builder_failure_context() helper, structured
  context on all 8 builder tests with unwrap_or_else + panic messages
- src/raptorq/gf256.rs: u64::from(c) replacing c as u64
- artifacts/raptorq_replay_catalog_v1.json: 8 new replay entries
- docs/raptorq_unit_test_matrix.md: updated line refs, status notes,
  gap descriptions narrowed to non-builder families, bd-61s90 → in_progress

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

-2. Some builder and GF256/property-style tests still rely on plain assertion text without schema-aligned key/value context.
-3. Matrix row status is still `partial` for builder/systematic/GF256 families until schema-aligned failure context is universal.
+2. Some non-builder GF256/property-style tests still rely on plain assertion text without schema-aligned key/value context.
+3. Matrix row status is still `partial` for systematic/GF256 families until schema-aligned failure context is universal.
 
 Mapped follow-up beads:
 
@@ -134,7 +134,7 @@ The D5 bead can close only when all of the following are true:
 
 | Bead | Scope | Current Status | Note for D5 Closure |
 |---|---|---|---|
-| `bd-61s90` | D5 comprehensive unit matrix | `open` | this matrix remains authoritative, but closure requires replay-id/schema completion on all required paths |
+| `bd-61s90` | D5 comprehensive unit matrix | `in_progress` | this matrix remains authoritative, but closure requires replay-id/schema completion on all required paths |
 | `bd-26pqk` | D9 replay catalog | `open` | catalog artifact exists; keep replay references aligned with latest entries |
 | `bd-oeql8` | D7 structured logging schema | `open` | schema contract enforcement still needed across all required suites |
 | `bd-3bvdj` | D6 deterministic E2E suite | `open` | unit-to-E2E linkage must remain synchronized as scenarios evolve |
